topic: Mem0 vs Zep in Production: Choosing the Right AI Agent Memory Framework
keyword: mem0 vs zep ai agent memory

key_points: Comprehensive 2026 comparison covering architecture, benchmarks, pricing, and self-hosting
LongMemEval benchmark: Zep 63.8% vs Mem0 49.0% using GPT-4o
Zep graph memory available at every tier ($25/mo); Mem0 graph locked behind $249/mo Pro
Mem0 self-hosting (Apache 2.0, OpenMemory) is mature; Zep Community Edition deprecated
Recommends Mem0 for personalized chatbots, Zep for temporal/entity reasoning
Zep outperforms Mem0 by 22+ percentage points on LongMemEval temporal reasoning tasks
Mem0 has largest community: ~51,800 GitHub stars, 21 framework integrations
Zep's credit-based model gives full feature access at every tier
Mem0 pricing: Free → $19/mo → $249/mo (graph only at Pro)
Mem0 enterprise: SOC 2, HIPAA, BYOK, on-prem deployment available
Five-way comparison including Letta, Supermemory, SuperLocalMemory
Mem0's April 2026 update: single-pass hierarchical extraction, multi-signal retrieval (semantic + BM25 + entity)
Mean tokens per retrieval: Mem0 ~6.7K-7.0K vs full-context baseline 25,000+
Zep retrieval latency under 200ms, optimized for voice AI
Mem0 achieves 26% relative improvement over OpenAI memory baseline
Mem0 dual-store: vector database for semantic search + knowledge graph for entity relationships
Zep Graphiti: temporal KG engine where time is first-class dimension with fact validity windows
Zep fuses chat and business data via single API call into unified Context Graph
Mem0 AWS partnership: exclusive memory provider for AWS Strands Agent SDK
CrewAI, Flowise, Langflow natively integrate Mem0
Mem0 supports user-level, session-level, agent-level memory scopes
Zep processes messages asynchronously — agent response time unimpacted
Zep auto-summarizes old chat history to keep prompts under token limits
Mem0 OpenMemory: MCP-compatible local server for Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf
Zep self-host requires Neo4j or FalkorDB/Kuzu graph database management

stats.fact: Zep scores 63.8% vs Mem0's 49.0% on LongMemEval benchmark with GPT-4o, a 15-point gap concentrated in temporal reasoning tasks
Zep reduces context tokens from 115,000 to 1,600 average, cutting response latency from 29-31 seconds to 2.5-3.2 seconds
stats.source: arxiv.org/abs/2501.13956 — Zep: A Temporal Knowledge Graph Architecture for Agent Memory

stats.fact: Mem0 has 51,800+ GitHub stars (Apache 2.0), 14 million Python package downloads, and 186 million API calls in Q3 2025
Mem0 raised $24M Series A and is used by Netflix, Lemonade, Rocket Money, and Fortune 500 companies in production
Mem0's updated April 2026 algorithm processes new memory turns in one LLM call with mean tokens ~6.7K-7.0K vs 25,000+ for full-context
stats.source: arxiv.org/abs/2504.19413 — Mem0: Building Production-Ready AI Agents with Scalable Long-Term Memory

stats.fact: Mem0 graph memory (Pro tier) costs $249/month vs Zep's Flex tier at $25/month for full graph access
Zep Graphiti shows 18.5% accuracy improvement over full-context and vector retrieval baselines on LongMemEval
Mem0 covers 21 official framework integrations across Python and TypeScript as of early 2026
Zep retrieves relevant context in under 200ms, optimized for voice AI and latency-sensitive applications
Mem0 achieves 26% relative improvement in LLM-as-a-Judge metric over OpenAI memory baseline

angles: The $249 vs $25 graph memory pricing cliff: why Mem0's feature gating is a hidden cost trap for production teams
Temporal reasoning as a first-class requirement: when Zep's 15-point benchmark advantage actually matters in production
Self-hosting reality check: Mem0 OpenMemory (mature, Docker-ready) vs Zep's deprecated Community Edition requiring Neo4j
The 'time as structure' vs 'time as metadata' architectural divide and how it shapes what each system can and can't do
Decision flowchart: chatbot personalization (Mem0) vs entity-relationship tracking over time (Zep)
Ecosystem lock-in analysis: Mem0's AWS Strands/CrewAI/Flowise integrations vs Zep's enterprise CRM specialization
Production migration path: what happens when you outgrow vector-only memory and need temporal graph capabilities
